- Network trust badges and verified seller profiles

- Full RLS policies and immutable audit logging
- Demo mode for all components when Supabase is offline

commit.message:
Scroll to top when advancing to next group in results reveal (#23)

When revealing results group-by-group, the page position wasn't reset
between groups, leaving users scrolled mid-page after advancing.

## Change

- `results-reveal.tsx`: call `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` alongside the state
increment in the "Reveal next group" button's `onClick`

```tsx
onClick={() => { setRevealedCount((c) => c + 1); window.scrollTo(0, 0); }}
```

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: valscion <482561+valscion@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: extract QueriesUserAccounts trait and FinAccountLot::computeMetrics (#209)

* refactor: extract QueriesUserAccounts trait and FinAccountLot::computeMetrics

Eliminates ~20 copies of the inline account-ownership check and the
repeated date-diff / gain-loss computation spread across the three
finance controllers.

- New `QueriesUserAccounts` trait (`Concerns/`) with `resolveOwnedAccount`
  and `getUserAccountIds` — applied to FinanceAccountsController,
  FinanceLotsController, and FinanceTransactionsApiController
- New `FinAccountLot::computeMetrics()` static method replaces duplicate
  purchase/sale date-diff and realized-gain-loss calculations in
  importLots and saveAnalyzedLots
- Removes all direct Auth::id() / FinAccounts::where('acct_owner') calls
  from the three controllers; each now delegates through the trait

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: finance:k1-migrate command + normalising parsed_data accessor (closes #204)

- New `finance:k1-migrate` artisan command backfills legacy flat-format K-1
  records to the canonical schemaVersion structure using K1LegacyTransformer.
  Supports --dry-run; idempotent (skips already-canonical records); scoped to
  FINANCE_CLI_USER_ID.

- Adds a normalising `parsedData` Eloquent Attribute accessor to
  FileForTaxDocument: on read, legacy K-1 parsed_data is transparently
  transformed to the canonical shape, so all consumers (MCP tools,
  future finance:tax-render) always see a single schema without branching.
  The setter JSON-encodes on write; the raw DB value is unchanged until the
  backfill command runs.

- Removes 'parsed_data' from $casts (superseded by the custom Attribute).

- 7 new feature tests covering migration, dry-run, idempotency, user scoping,
  and the model accessor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all PR review comments - type safety, query scope, skipped counters, env cleanup

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bherila/2025-website/sessions/5b4fe334-c082-4eef-87a7-983a3efe789c

commit.message:
fix: ensure fresh clone setup works end-to-end

- sync_env.py: Create config.json from config.example.json if missing
  (was silently skipping on fresh clone since config.json is .gitignored)
- setup_jetson.sh: Move provisioning to step 8 (before model download)
  so Supabase credentials are available for ONNX model download in step 9.
  Support 3 credential sources: provisioning token, manual entry, env vars.
- setup_windows_dev.bat: Always run sync_env.py regardless of .env source
  (was skipping when .env.example was used)
- Update jetson_deployment_guide.md to reflect new step ordering

commit.message:
📊 Daily trending report 2026-04-12

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Show notice when variant data unavailable for a set (#40)

* Revert variant inference — only expand from actual price data

Don't guess at variants when TCGPlayer has no data. Sets without
price data (new/unreleased) show one entry per card number. Variant
expansion only happens when the API actually reports multiple price
types. We show what we know, not what we assume.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show notice when variant data unavailable from TCGPlayer

When >50% of loaded cards lack TCGPlayer pricing (and thus variant
data), show a notice: 'Variant data (reverse holo, etc.) is not yet
available for this set from TCGPlayer.' This honestly communicates
the limitation for new sets instead of silently showing incomplete
results or guessing at variants.

Reverts the rarity-based inference approach — we only expand variants
from actual TCGPlayer price type data.

Bumps CARD_CACHE_VERSION to 3 to clear stale data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
User/yashasg/brower client (#18)

* fix: enable keyboard input and fix canvas scaling for WASM builds

- Add PLATFORM_WEB as PUBLIC define on shapeshifter_lib for Emscripten
  so gesture_system.cpp (compiled in the lib) sees the flag
- Change #ifdef PLATFORM_DESKTOP to include PLATFORM_WEB for keyboard
  fields (input.h), key reading (input_system), and key→gesture
  translation (gesture_system)
- Fix HTML shell: add max-width/max-height + object-fit:contain so the
  720×1280 canvas scales to fit the viewport instead of overflowing
- Change canvas tabindex from -1 to 0 and auto-focus on load/click so
  keyboard and mouse events are received reliably

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rename WASM output to index.html and add responsive canvas layout

- Set OUTPUT_NAME to 'index' for Emscripten so the build produces
  index.html (servers serve this by default)
- Mobile (touch devices): canvas fills the full screen with
  object-fit:contain
- Desktop (pointer devices): canvas scales to viewport height with
  fixed 9:16 aspect ratio, centered horizontally
- Disable all scrolling on both platforms (overflow:hidden,
  touch-action:none, touchmove preventDefault)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: deploy WASM build to GitHub Pages on main push

- Fix artifact paths from shapeshifter.* to index.* (matches
  OUTPUT_NAME change)
- Add deploy job that runs after successful build on main branch
- Uses actions/upload-pages-artifact + actions/deploy-pages for
  static site deployment
- Only deploys on push to main (skipped for PRs)

Note: GitHub Pages must be configured to deploy from GitHub Actions
in the repository Settings > Pages > Source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: readability pass — deduplicate code and unify platform macros

Consolidate duplicated code into shared headers:

- enum_names.h: canonical shape_name(), obstacle_kind_name(),
  timing_tier_name() — replaces 6 static copies across 3 files
- platform.h: single PLATFORM_HAS_KEYBOARD macro — replaces
  scattered #if defined(PLATFORM_DESKTOP) || defined(PLATFORM_WEB)
- platform_utils.h: safe_localtime(), safe_fopen() — replaces
  identical #ifdef _WIN32 wrappers in file_logger.cpp,
  session_logger.cpp, and main.cpp

Extract tick_fixed_systems() in main.cpp so the 18-system loop is
defined once instead of copy-pasted across native and Emscripten paths.

Remove unused SwipeGesture::Tap enum value (never referenced).

Net: -111 lines, zero behavior change, all 383 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: implement architecture improvements from DoD review

1. Split rhythm.h into three headers by access pattern:
   - beat_map.h: loaded-once data (BeatEntry, BeatMap) — includes <string>, <vector>
   - song_state.h: runtime singletons (SongState, HPState, SongResults)
   - rhythm.h: per-entity components (TimingGrade, BeatInfo) + timing helpers
   rhythm.h re-exports both for backward compatibility.

2. Extract begin_shape_window() in player_action_system to eliminate
   the duplicated 9-line window-init block (Idle and Active+different
   branches were identical).

3. Extract scene-draw helpers from render_system (456→~450 lines but
   now 5 focused functions): draw_title_scene, draw_hud,
   draw_game_over_overlay, draw_song_complete_overlay, draw_pause_overlay.

4. Move enter_playing() into setup_play_session() (play_session.h/cpp),
   separating entity construction from the game state machine.

5. Single-pass collision: replace 5 separate entt::view iterations with
   one view + switch on Obstacle::kind. Simpler code, one iteration.

All 383 tests pass, zero behavior change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split PlayerShape into PlayerShape + ShapeWindow

PlayerShape (12B) now holds only render-path data: current, previous,
morph_t. ShapeWindow (28B) holds rhythm-mode timing data: target_shape,
phase_raw, window_timer, window_start, peak_time, window_scale, graded.

Systems that only need the current shape (render_system,
player_movement_system) no longer pull in 28 bytes of window timing
data. Systems that manage the window (shape_window_system,
collision_system, player_action_system) query both components.

Also removed spurious PlayerShape from burnout_system's view — it
queried the component but never read any field from it.

All 383 tests pass (803 assertions).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Full-bleed active song background (#20)

The active playing song highlight was constrained to the `max-w-2xl`
container. This makes it break out edge-to-edge across the viewport.

- **`vote-song-list.tsx`**: Replace `before:-inset-0.5
before:rounded-2xl` with the full-bleed pattern `before:-inset-y-0.5
before:left-[calc(-50vw+50%)] before:w-screen` — removes rounded corners
and spans the gradient to viewport edges
- **`document.tsx`**: Add `overflow-x-hidden` on `<body>` to suppress
horizontal scrollbar from the `100vw` pseudo-element

![Active song
full-bleed](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7512db0d-86e8-47fe-88f8-b54887e93a5d)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Response body consumed error: dedup via parsed JSON not Response (#39)

The request deduplication cached Promise<Response> and tried to
clone it for subsequent callers. But Response.clone() fails after
the body is consumed by the first caller's .json() call.

Fix: add scheduledFetchJson<T>() that caches the parsed JSON promise
instead. All callers share the same parsed result with no body
consumption issues. Updated all call sites in pokemonTcgApi.ts and
pokeApi.ts to use scheduledFetchJson.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: improve benchmark with warm-up and cache stats

- Add warm-up iteration to eliminate cold-start noise from measurements
- Increase iterations from 10 to 20 for better p95 accuracy
- Report actual cache hit/miss/eviction statistics
- Clarify measurement methodology in output

Results show excellent cache performance:
- 95% cache hit rate (100/105 queries)
- Sub-millisecond response times for cached queries
- Consistent P95 latency across multiple runs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

2. Update stale meta description (was still 'Free tools')
3. Add toolkit pages + blog posts to search index (index.json)

build(nightly): build Svelte web-ui before iradio-bridge Rust binary

The binary uses rust_embed to bake web-ui/dist/ in at compile time.
Without running 'npm ci && npm run build' first, the embedded UI is just
a stub index.html with no JS/CSS assets — resulting in a blank page.

Added setup-node v22 + npm ci + npm run build steps before cargo build.

* fix: enhance layer name validation in BaseBackwardBuilder to check against both layers and others

* test: ensure the correctness of unit tests

* fix: correct computation of x_g in GlobalResponseNorm to use jnp.sqrt

* fix: update computation of x_g in GlobalResponseNormalization to use sqrt of squared inputs

* test: Add comprehensive tests for various model configurations and behaviors

- Enhance test coverage for BackwardBehavior and LayerBehavior with additional validation scenarios.
- Introduce tests for UserDefinedBackward to ensure proper handling of inputs, outputs, and mixed precision configurations.
- Implement tests for model creation commands in Flax, Keras, and Torch, validating generated scripts against expected structures.
- Add tests for GlobalResponseNorm in both Flax and Keras, ensuring output consistency with timm's implementation.
- Extend trainer tests to include device management and input creation functionalities.
- Introduce NamedCallbackList tests to validate callback registration and management.
- Enhance optimizer tests to cover new scheduler functionalities and ensure proper integration with learning rate scaling.

* feat: add CycleGAN generator and discriminator configuration files

* feat: add CycleGAN configuration for generator and discriminator training

* docs: update documentation for CycleGAN support in backward templates and training workflows

commit.message:
feat: add terminal simple logger for test scenarios (#7)

Add a `terminal_logger` module that prints coloured one-liners to stdout
when test scenarios start/end:
- ▶ scenario-name (default colour, test started)
- ✓ scenario-name (green, test passed)
- ✗ scenario-name — error (red, test failed)

Opt-in via `LuluClientConfig::terminal_logger` (default: false).
Integrated into lulu_beg_test_scenario() and lulu_end_test_scenario().

commit.message:
修复并发写入：users.json 关键写操作加 flock 文件锁

- 新增 EZ_USERS_LOCK / EZ_STATE_LOCK 锁文件常量
- 新增 acquire_lock / release_lock 辅助函数（flock -w 10，超时报错）
- traffic_collect（cron 入口）: 持锁覆盖整个 for 循环
- add_user / del_user / toggle_user: 写入前 acquire，写入后 release
- reset_user_uuid: 持锁覆盖全部批量写操作（删旧/写新/更密码/重置计数）
- traffic_set_limit 四个分支 / traffic_reset_user 两个分支: 各自加锁
- traffic_check_limit 在 traffic_collect 锁内调用，无需单独加锁

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: make GHCR packages public after push so Railway can pull images

After each image build+push, call GitHub REST API to set container
package visibility to public. Railway cannot authenticate to private
GHCR packages and silently rolls back to the old container when the
image pull fails with 401.

Using GITHUB_TOKEN (packages: write permission already declared in
workflow) to PATCH /orgs/{owner}/packages/container/{pkg} with
visibility=public. The step is non-fatal — warns on failure but does
not block the deploy trigger.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: overhaul setup scripts (venv-first dev, Docker Jetson)

Dev setup (setup_dev.sh, setup_windows_dev.bat):
- Require venv to be active BEFORE running (check VIRTUAL_ENV)
- Enforce Python 3.10 (not 3.10+)
- All pip installs go into active venv, not system Python
- Clear repo-relative paths and quick start instructions

Jetson setup (setup_jetson.sh):
- Docker-based headless REST API (nvidia L4T container)
- System Python + apt for GUI (no venv, avoids PyQt5 pip issues)
- Self-contained: no setup_dev.sh dependency
- 13-step flow: platform check, Docker build, system packages,
  ONNX download, TensorRT conversion, systemd services, provisioning
- Add .dockerignore to exclude models/data from build context

Other changes:
- Dockerfile: use requirements.txt COPY instead of inline pip
- requirements.txt: opencv-python-headless (not full GUI variant)
- ruff.toml: target py310 (was py38)
- Docs updated: jetson_deployment_guide.md, project_instruction.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update smb4_mlb_ratings/ingest/pitch_quality.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add weekly exercise group planner with cosine-centroid classification

Backend:
- New exercise_groups.py module with cosine-centroid classifier
  - 6 groups (Push, Pull, Legs, Shoulders, Arms, Core) defined as
    region-weight centroids
  - Exercises classified by cosine similarity to nearest centroid
  - Hardcoded weekly schedule: Tue Push+Core, Wed Legs, Thu Pull+Arms,
    Sat Shoulders+Push+Core, Sun Legs+Arms, Mon+Fri rest
  - Uses canonicalize_region() from tissue_regions.py for region
    normalization
  - Batched freshness queries (days_since_trained, RPE counts)
- New GET /api/planner/weekly-menu endpoint
- 22 tests covering cosine similarity, classification, region profiles,
  weekly menu structure, and endpoint

Frontend:
- StrengthPlannerCard now shows M T W Th F Sa Su day selector
- Each day shows exercises organized by training group with color-coded
  headers
- Exercises sorted by recency (recently trained first)
- Cross-day selection persistence: checking exercises from one day keeps
  them checked when switching to another day
- Rest days (Mon, Fri) show a rest day message
- Cross-day selection count badge

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(s05): Choice & Consequence narrative surfacing, divergence steering, dormant fix (#112)

* feat(s05): choice & consequence narrative surfacing, divergence steering, dormant fix

Wave 25: S05 Choice & Consequence remediation (~40% → ~65-70%)

- AC-5.1: Inject active consequence details into generation prompt so
  narrator weaves manifesting consequences into the narrative
- AC-5.4: Permanent choice signals — when classifier detects PERMANENT
  reversibility, inject external environmental signals (atmosphere,
  NPC reactions, stakes) without narrating player thoughts
- AC-5.8: Fix dormant detection bug (last_active_turn updated on every
  scan, preventing chains from ever going dormant); wire prune_chains()
  into understand_stage; return closure descriptions for narrative hints
- AC-5.10: Wire calculate_divergence() into context_stage; store
  prompt-ready divergence_guidance on TurnState; inject steering when
  divergence is high
- Performance benchmarks: evaluate <300ms for 30 chains, divergence
  <100ms, prune <100ms

Production changes:
- consequence_service.py: Fix last_active_turn bug, prune_chains()
  returns tuple[list[UUID], list[str]] with closure descriptions
- generate.py: Consequence surfacing, permanent signals, divergence
  guidance, closure hints in prompt
- context.py: Divergence calculation after active chain retrieval
- understand.py: Chain pruning as pipeline step 6
- turn.py: Add divergence_guidance and pruned_chain_closures fields

Tests: 18 new (15 functional + 3 performance benchmarks)
Fixes 2 existing tests for prune_chains return type change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address copilot review comments for wave 25

- Fix anchor selection to use nearest_anchor_id from DivergenceScore
- Add turn parameter to reveal_hidden_entry to update last_active_turn
- Make no-anchors test assert explicitly (divergence=0.0 when no anchors)
- Add @pytest.mark.slow to performance benchmarks for CI resilience

commit.message:
feat: macOS wallpaper, HIG polish, caching, dev infra

- Add macOS wallpaper gesture (long press / right-click) via posix_spawn + AppleScript
- Fix 8 code review findings: AppleScript injection, race condition in load(),
  memory leak, double setTaskCompleted, force-unwrap URLs, DateFormatter churn,
  weak self guard, silent try?
- HIG improvements: material-backed overlays, safe-area-driven layout,
  Reduce Motion support, semantic colors, accessible error overlay,
  faster animations (.snappy/.easeIn), labeled ProgressView
- Optimize Cloudflare costs: shared URLCache, prefetch history in background,
  cache-first wallpaper download, screen saver 60s interval
- Smart history navigation: anchor to latest artwork date from API,
  7-day sliding window, arrow key support on Mac
- Dev infra: SwiftLint config + CI job, code coverage in CI,
  copilot-setup-steps.yml, Xcode 26.2 in CI, updated README
- Add 3 new tests (canGoBack, boundary guard), total 29 tests passing

Security & cleanup: XSS fixes, orphaned CSS removal, webkit prefixes

SECURITY (Critical + Medium):
- C1: Fix stored XSS in feedback.js — esc() on GitHub comment bodies
- M1: Add esc() to innerHTML in 5 tool JS files (deptime, cert-tracker,
  licensing, ai-mapper, copilot-matrix) — all data fields now escaped
- M3: Fix search.js innerHTML — escape title, url, tags, dates

CODE CLEANUP:
- M10: Consolidate 4 fixed pseudo-layers → 2 (remove .is-home overrides,
  use premium values site-wide)
- M11: Fix broken HTML on Free Tools page (stray div)
- L1: Remove ~200 lines of orphaned CSS (old hp-tool-card, hp-search-box,
  welcome-ctas, channel-card, hp-stats-bar rules)
- L2: Remove dead 480px CTA media query rules
- L3: Add -webkit-backdrop-filter prefix to all backdrop-filter rules
- L4: Fix missing noreferrer on target=_blank links across 11 templates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update src/snippy_ng/stages/reporting.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Extract GraphQL validation, add second-pass retry, refactor to eliminate duplication (#71)

* Extract GraphQL response validation into testable module

Extract the inline response parsing/validation logic from
Update-Maintainers.ps1 into GraphQLHelper.psm1 with a
Test-GraphQLResponse function. Add 14 Pester tests covering:
- Valid responses (with and without results)
- GraphQL body errors (single, multiple, partial data)
- Missing/malformed structure (no data, no search, empty, truncated)
- StrictMode safety regression tests (the exact bug that broke main)

All tests run under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest to catch
property-access bugs like the PSObject.Properties issue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add second-pass retry for repos that fail all attempts

When a repo exhausts all 5 retry attempts (e.g. persistent HTTP 502),
queue it for a second pass after all other repos are processed. The
natural delay from processing remaining repos plus a 60s cooldown gives
GitHub's API time to recover from transient outages.

Motivated by dotnet/dotnet-api-docs failing all 5 attempts with HTTP 502
while dotnet/maui, dotnet/roslyn, and dotnet/sdk recovered on retry 2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix GraphQLHelper.psm1 review comments: update help text and fix PSObject guard

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/danmoseley/pr-dashboard/sessions/8add3188-cb08-484e-993d-98576eb3145c

Co-authored-by: danmoseley <6385855+danmoseley@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor: extract Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan to eliminate duplication

Extract the per-repo scan logic (query building, pagination, retry/backoff,
response validation, node processing, activity collection) into a reusable
Invoke-RepoMaintainerScan function. Both the first pass and second-pass
retry now call this function, eliminating ~100 lines of duplicated code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix dedup bug: use Sort-Object -Unique instead of Select-Object -Unique

Select-Object -Unique only removes adjacent duplicates, so non-adjacent
duplicate logins could survive into maintainers.json. Sort-Object -Unique
does a proper global dedup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
